<|file_sep|>README.md
# Multilingual-Text-Detection

This project is a multilingual text detection model that can detect text in 100+ languages. The model is based on the [PaddleOCR](https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR) and [PaddleDetection](https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleDetection) frameworks.

## Features

- Detect text in 100+ languages
- High accuracy and speed
- Easy to use and deploy

## Installation

1. Clone the repository:
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/yourusername/multilingual-text-detection.git
   ```

2. Install the required dependencies:
   ```bash
   pip install -r requirements.txt
   ```

## Usage

1. Prepare your input images.

2. Run the detection script:
   ```bash
   python detect.py --image_path path/to/your/image.jpg
   ```

3. The detected text will be saved in the output directory.

## Model

The model is trained on a large dataset of multilingual text images. It uses a combination of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and recurrent neural networks (RNNs) to detect and recognize text in images.

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please feel free to submit a pull request.

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License.

"""Tokenization classes for RoFormer."""

import collections
import os
import unicodedata
from typing import List, Optional, Tuple

from ...tokenization_utils import PreTrainedTokenizer, _is_control, _is_punctuation, _is_whitespace
from ...utils import logging


logger = logging.get_logger(__name__)

VOCAB_FILES_NAMES = {"vocab_file": "vocab.txt"}

        "junnyu/roformer_chinese_base": "https://huggingface.co/junnyu/roformer_chinese_base/resolve/main/vocab.txt",

        "junnyu/roformer_small_generator": "https://huggingface.co/junnyu/roformer_small_generator/resolve/main/vocab.txt",
    }
}

PRETRAINED_POSITIONAL_EMBEDDINGS_SIZES = {
    "junnyu/roformer_chinese_small": 1536,
    "junnyu/roformer_chinese_base": 1536,
    "junnyu/roformer_chinese_char_base": 512,
    "junnyu/roformer_small_discriminator": 128,
    "junnyu/roformer_small_generator": 128,
}


# Copied from transformers.models.bert.tokenization_bert.load_vocab
def load_vocab(vocab_file):
    """Loads a vocabulary file into a dictionary."""
    vocab = collections.OrderedDict()
    with open(vocab_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as reader:
        tokens = reader.readlines()
    for index, token in enumerate(tokens):
        token = token.rstrip("\n")
        vocab[token] = index
    return vocab


# Copied from transformers.models.bert.tokenization_bert.whitespace_tokenize
def whitespace_tokenize(text):
    """Runs basic whitespace cleaning and splitting on a piece of text."""
    text = text.strip()
    if not text:
        return []
    tokens = text.split()
    return tokens


# Copied from transformers.models.bert.tokenization_bert.BasicTokenizer
class BasicTokenizer(object):
    """
    Constructs a BasicTokenizer that will run basic tokenization (punctuation splitting, lower casing, etc.).

    Args:
        do_lower_case (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `True`):
            Whether or not to lowercase the input when tokenizing.
        never_split (`Iterable`, *optional*):
            Collection of tokens which will never be split during tokenization. Only has an effect when
            `do_basic_tokenize=True`
        tokenize_chinese_chars (`bool`, *optional*, defaults to `True`):
            Whether or not to tokenize Chinese characters.

            This should likely be deactivated for Japanese (see this
            [issue](https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/issues/328)).
        strip_accents: (`bool`, *optional*):
            Whether or not to strip all accents. If this option is not specified, then it will be determined by the
            value for `lowercase` (as in the original BERT).
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        do_lower_case=True,
        never_split=None,
        tokenize_chinese_chars=True,
        strip_accents=None,
    ):
        if never_split is None:
            never_split = []
        self.do_lower_case = do_lower_case

# 🌐 Multilingual Text Classification

This repository contains the code and resources for the paper **"Multilingual Text Classification: A Comprehensive Survey"**.

## 📄 Paper

You can access the paper [here](https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.14446).

## 📚 Dataset

The dataset used in this study can be accessed [here](https://huggingface.co/datasets/ChenxiChen/Multilingual-Text-Classification).

## 📊 Results

The results of our experiments can be found [here](https://huggingface.co/datasets/ChenxiChen/Multilingual-Text-Classification/blob/main/results.csv).

## 🤗 Hugging Face

You can find our models on Hugging Face [here](https://huggingface.co/ChenxiChen).

## 📝 Citation

If you use this work, please cite our paper:

```bibtex
@article{chen2024multilingual,
  title={Multilingual Text Classification: A Comprehensive Survey},
  author={Chen, Chenxi and others},
  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2402.14446},
  year={2024}
}
```

# CodeBERT

This is the repository for the paper "[CodeBERT: A Pre-Trained Model for Programming and Natural Languages](https://arxiv.org/abs/2002.08155)".

CodeBERT is a bimodal pre-trained model for programming language (PL) and natural language (NL). CodeBERT learns general-purpose representations that support downstream NL-PL tasks such as natural language code search, code documentation generation, etc. 

CodeBERT is developed by mixing high-resource NL-PL pairs (e.g., Python-Documentation pairs from GitHub) with NL-NL pairs (e.g., English-English pairs from Wikipedia). CodeBERT is trained using the masked language modeling objective on 6 million GitHub repositories and 4 million documentation pairs.

We evaluate CodeBERT on NL-PL tasks such as code search and code documentation generation. For code search, we fine-tune CodeBERT on the [CodeSearchNet dataset](https://github.com/github/CodeSearchNet). For code documentation generation, we fine-tune CodeBERT on the [CONCODE dataset](https://github.com/salesforce/ConCode). Experimental results show that CodeBERT achieves state-of-the-art results on both tasks.

## Pre-trained Model

We provide the pre-trained CodeBERT model in PyTorch format. You can download the model from [here](https://huggingface.co/microsoft/codebert-base/tree/main).

## Fine-tuning

We provide the code for fine-tuning CodeBERT on the CodeSearchNet dataset for code search and the CONCODE dataset for code documentation generation.

### Code Search

To fine-tune CodeBERT on the CodeSearchNet dataset, run the following command:

```bash
python run_classifier.py \
    --model_type roberta \
    --model_name_or_path microsoft/codebert-base \
    --train_data_file train.csv \
    --eval_data_file test.csv \
    --output_dir /tmp/test-cs/ \
    --max_seq_length 256 \
    --train_batch_size 32 \
    --eval_batch_size 32 \
    --learning_rate 2e-5 \
    --num_train_epochs 10 \
    --do_train \
    --do_eval \
    --evaluate_during_training \
    --seed 123
```

### Code Documentation Generation

To fine-tune CodeBERT on the CONCODE dataset, run the following command:

```bash
python run_seq2seq.py \
    --model_type roberta \
    --model_name_or_path microsoft/codebert-base \
    --train_data_file train.json \
    --eval_data_file test.json \
    --output_dir /tmp/test-cdg/ \
    --max_source_length 256 \
    --max_target_length 128 \
    --train_batch_size 32 \
    --eval_batch_size 32 \
    --learning_rate 5e-5 \
    --num_train_epochs 30 \
    --do_train \
    --do_eval \
    --evaluate_during_training \
    --seed 123
```

## Evaluation

We provide the code for evaluating CodeBERT on the CodeSearchNet dataset for code search and the CONCODE dataset for code documentation generation.

### Code Search

To evaluate CodeBERT on the CodeSearchNet dataset, run the following command:

```bash
python run_classifier.py \
    --model_type roberta \
    --model_name_or_path microsoft/codebert-base \
    --train_data_file train.csv \
    --eval_data_file test.csv \
    --output_dir /tmp/test-cs/ \
    --max_seq_length 256 \
    --train_batch_size 32 \
    --eval_batch_size 32 \
    --learning_rate 2e-5 \
    --num_train_epochs 10 \
    --do_eval \
    --seed 123
```

### Code Documentation Generation

To evaluate CodeBERT on the CONCODE dataset, run the following command:

```bash
python run_seq2seq.py \
    --model_type roberta \
    --model_name_or_path microsoft/codebert-base \
    --train_data_file train.json \
    --eval_data_file test.json \
    --output_dir /tmp/test-cdg/ \
    --max_source_length 256 \
    --max_target_length 128 \
    --train_batch_size 32 \
    --eval_batch_size 32 \
    --learning_rate 5e-5 \
    --num_train_epochs 30 \
    --do_eval \
    --seed 123
```
